scopeItems.label: G1: Alignment with relevant EU regulations (CSRD, ESRS, EED, ESPR, WEEE, LCA, ISO14040, ISO14044)
G2: Existence of a formal environmental sustainability strategy
G3: Defined roles and responsibilities for sustainability
G4: Inclusion of sustainability in internal policies or quality systems
G5: Inclusion of sustainability in procurement or supplier management
S1: Sustainability awareness/training for staff
S2: Involvement of stakeholders in environmental planning
S3: Transparent communication of environmental performance
E1: Adoption of renewable energy sources
E2: AI-driven workload or energy optimisation
E3: Waste heat recovery or reuse strategies
E4: Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) tracking and optimisation
E5: Carbon Use Effectiveness (CUE) tracking and reduction
E6: Flexible energy usage or smart grid responsiveness
M1: Measurement of environmental KPIs (GHG, energy, water, waste)
M2: Use of environmental metrics in planning and reporting
M3: Lifecycle-based environmental impact assessment (based on LCA methodology)
M4: Use of WUE (Water Usage Effectiveness) or similar water metrics
M5: Implementation of monitoring tools/platforms
P1: Reduction of operational waste (incl. e-waste)
P2: End-of-life management for equipment
P3: Circular IT practices (reuse, remanufacturing, modularity)
P4: Pollution control (air, water, soil)
P5: Use of low-impact or non-toxic materials
W1: Water usage monitoring and reduction strategies
W2: Impact on local ecosystems or biodiversity
W3: Rainwater harvesting, reuse, or runoff management
W4: Water-based cooling systems and water efficiency strategies
L1: Sustainability embedded in the design phase
L2: Consideration of sustainability during implementation
L3: Operational sustainability practices
L4: Decommissioning/termination phase planning

questions.text: To what extent is the RI aligned with relevant EU environmental regulations such as CSRD, ESRS, EED, ESPR, WEEE, LCA, ISO14040, ISO14044?
questions.reference: CSRD, ESRS E1-E5,  ESPR, EED, WEEE, LCA, ISO14040, ISO14044

answerOptions.description: The RI is not actively considering EU environmental regulations or is unaware of specific obligations.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Conduct a regulatory gap analysis to identify which EU environmental regulations apply and where the RI is currently non-compliant. Engage legal or compliance expertise to support interpretation if needed.

answerOptions.description: The RI is aware of applicable regulations and has initiated some exploratory or preparatory activities, but there is no structured compliance plan.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Update internal documentation and procedures to close identified regulatory gaps. Begin implementing key compliance actions with clear timelines and responsibilities.

answerOptions.description: The RI has mapped relevant regulations, assigned responsibilities, and embedded compliance requirements into formal policies and operational procedures.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No further action required. Maintain alignment by reviewing regulatory changes annually and updating internal policies accordingly.

questions.text: Has the RI conducted a review or gap analysis to identify which regulations apply and how they are addressed?

answerOptions.description: No structured review or gap analysis has been conducted; awareness of obligations is minimal or outdated.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Initiate a structured regulatory review to identify applicable EU regulations (e.g. CSRD, ESRS, EED, ESPR, WEEE). Engage relevant internal or external expertise and document initial findings.

answerOptions.description: A basic review has been done; some gaps identified, but follow-up actions are limited or informal.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand and update the regulatory gap analysis. Clarify which obligations are partially addressed, and prepare an action plan to resolve any gaps identified.

answerOptions.description: A comprehensive, up-to-date gap analysis is in place; regularly reviewed and informs compliance actions.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain and periodically update the gap analysis in line with evolving legislation and internal priorities.

questions.text: Is there a documented environmental sustainability strategy in place, endorsed by leadership and periodically updated?

answerOptions.description: No environmental sustainability strategy exists, or it is informal and not institutionally endorsed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Draft a documented environmental sustainability strategy that outlines goals, scope, and key priorities. Secure leadership engagement to initiate endorsement.

answerOptions.description: A strategy exists and covers key areas, but it is not comprehensive or regularly reviewed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Formalise leadership endorsement of the strategy and ensure it includes a mechanism for periodic updates. Begin integrating it into RI-level decision-making.

answerOptions.description: A formal strategy is in place, endorsed by leadership, reviewed regularly, and linked to operational planning.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain periodic reviews and ensure the strategy remains aligned with evolving sustainability goals and regulations.

questions.text: Are roles and responsibilities for environmental sustainability clearly assigned across relevant functions within the RI?

answerOptions.description: No roles or responsibilities are defined for sustainability-related actions.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify key functions and assign clear responsibilities for environmental sustainability. Ensure responsibility is not siloed but shared across operational and strategic roles.

answerOptions.description: Some roles are assigned, but responsibilities are not clearly documented or consistently applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Document the assigned responsibilities formally (e.g. in organisational charts, role descriptions, quality systems) and ensure they are communicated internally.

answerOptions.description: Roles and responsibilities are formally assigned, documented, and integrated into operational procedures.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Sustain clarity and alignment of responsibilities, and update assignments when organisational roles or sustainability goals evolve.

questions.text: To what extent is environmental sustainability integrated into the RI’s internal policies, management systems, or quality frameworks?

answerOptions.description: Environmental considerations are not referenced in internal policies or management systems.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Review existing policies, management systems, and quality frameworks to identify where environmental sustainability is absent. Begin drafting updates or addenda that reflect sustainability considerations.

answerOptions.description: Sustainability is mentioned in some policies or quality processes, but integration is limited or inconsistent.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand integration of sustainability elements across internal systems. Ensure alignment with recognised frameworks such as ISO 14001 or ISO 9001 (with sustainability add-ons), and involve relevant departments in updates.

answerOptions.description: Environmental sustainability is systematically integrated across internal policies, quality management, and procedures.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue internal reviews to maintain full integration and alignment with emerging sustainability standards.

questions.text: Does the RI include environmental sustainability criteria in procurement processes and supplier evaluations?
questions.associatedMetric: % of suppliers evaluated with environmental criteria (optional)
questions.reference: ISO 20400, ESRS G1, ESRS E5, EU Green Public Procurement Guidelines

answerOptions.description: Environmental criteria are not considered in procurement or supplier evaluation.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Begin integrating basic environmental sustainability criteria into procurement guidelines and supplier selection processes. Reference relevant standards (e.g. EU Green Public Procurement) where applicable.

answerOptions.description: Some environmental criteria are used, but not systematically or across all procurement categories.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand and formalise sustainability criteria in procurement documents and supplier contracts. Ensure criteria are measurable and consistently applied across purchases.

answerOptions.description: Environmental sustainability is consistently integrated into procurement processes and supplier assessments.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain criteria updates and consider supplier engagement strategies to support continuous sustainability improvement.

questions.text: To what extent does the RI provide staff training or awareness programmes on environmental sustainability topics relevant to their roles?
questions.associatedMetric: % of staff trained annually, number of training sessions

answerOptions.description: No sustainability training or awareness activities are in place.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Develop and deliver basic sustainability awareness sessions for staff. Tailor content to general operational relevance and RI objectives.

answerOptions.description: Some ad hoc or optional training is available, but not systematic.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Establish structured training programmes with role-specific content. Include periodic refreshers and track staff participation and feedback.

answerOptions.description: Structured, role-specific sustainability training is delivered regularly and tracked.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain comprehensive training programmes and consider integrating sustainability awareness into onboarding and performance reviews.

questions.text: Are relevant stakeholders (e.g. users, suppliers, partners) engaged in environmental planning or sustainability goal-setting processes?

answerOptions.description: Stakeholders are not engaged in environmental planning or goal setting.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify key stakeholder groups and initiate basic engagement on environmental topics (e.g. surveys, meetings, informal consultations).

answerOptions.description: Stakeholder input is sought occasionally but not structured or consistently applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Develop a structured stakeholder engagement plan that includes regular input into sustainability planning or goal-setting processes. Document outcomes and integrate feedback.

answerOptions.description: Stakeholders are regularly and systematically involved in environmental planning processes.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue proactive stakeholder engagement and track participation and influence on environmental decisions.

questions.text: Does the RI regularly communicate its environmental performance, actions, or goals to internal and external stakeholders?
questions.associatedMetric: Frequency and scope of communications (e.g., reports/year)

answerOptions.description: Environmental performance is not communicated externally or internally.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify key performance metrics and develop internal channels for sharing environmental information (e.g. newsletters, intranet updates).

answerOptions.description: Some communications occur, but are informal or irregular.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify key performance metrics and develop internal channels for sharing environmental information (e.g. newsletters, intranet updates).

answerOptions.description: Environmental performance is communicated regularly, transparently, and in structured formats.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Establish regular reporting cycles and communication plans for both internal and external audiences. Include key performance indicators, progress on goals, and future plans.

questions.text: What proportion of the RI’s energy consumption is sourced from renewable energy, and how is this verified?
questions.reference: ISO 50001, ESRS E1, EU Renewable Energy Directive

answerOptions.description: The RI does not use or track renewable energy usage.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Assess the current energy mix and identify opportunities to procure or transition to renewable energy sources. Start collecting data and documentation to verify sources (e.g. supplier declarations, energy certificates).

answerOptions.description: Some energy comes from renewable sources, but tracking is limited or informal.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Increase the share of renewable energy through updated contracts or on-site generation. Implement processes for regular verification and reporting (e.g. through Guarantees of Origin, utility audits).

answerOptions.description: A high proportion of energy is sourced from renewables, verified through formal documentation or contracts.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue monitoring and ensure transparency in reporting renewable energy sourcing and verification.

questions.text: Are AI or automation systems used to optimise energy consumption across compute, cooling, or other infrastructure components?

answerOptions.description: No AI or automation tools are in place for energy optimisation.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify infrastructure areas with high energy use and evaluate the potential of AI or automation tools for energy optimisation. Initiate pilot projects or feasibility assessments.

answerOptions.description: Some systems exist, but usage is limited to specific components or lacks ongoing refinement.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand use of AI-driven or automated systems for energy optimisation in key areas (e.g. dynamic workload distribution, smart cooling). Document energy savings and lessons learned.

answerOptions.description: Advanced AI/automation is used systematically across operations to monitor and optimise energy use.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain optimisation systems and review performance periodically to identify further improvement opportunities.

questions.text: Does the RI implement strategies to recover or reuse waste heat generated by infrastructure?
questions.associatedMetric: Energy reuse factor (ERF), % of recovered heat reused

answerOptions.description: No waste heat recovery strategies are in place or considered.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Assess the feasibility of heat recovery based on infrastructure type and thermal load. Identify potential use cases (e.g. heating buildings, district heating, internal reuse).

answerOptions.description: Opportunities have been identified, and partial recovery is implemented in limited areas.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement basic heat recovery systems where feasible. Monitor effectiveness and explore opportunities to expand reuse or integrate with external systems.

answerOptions.description: Waste heat is consistently captured and reused through formal systems and integrated planning.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue optimising heat recovery systems and track performance against energy-saving targets.

questions.text: Is the RI’s PUE monitored regularly, and are efforts in place to optimise it over time?
questions.reference: ISO/IEC 30134-2, EN 50600-4-2, EU CoC on Data Centre Energy Efficiency

answerOptions.description: PUE is not monitored or known.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Begin monitoring Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) at a basic level. Install or configure metering infrastructure to enable regular data collection.

answerOptions.description: PUE is monitored but not regularly optimised or benchmarked.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Establish routine monitoring of PUE and identify areas where efficiency can be improved (e.g. cooling systems, power distribution). Set internal targets for optimisation.

answerOptions.description: PUE is tracked, benchmarked, and used to inform continuous energy efficiency improvements.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain performance tracking and review optimisation efforts to stay aligned with industry benchmarks and internal goals.

questions.text: Is CUE calculated and used as a metric for assessing carbon performance of the RI’s operations?

answerOptions.description: CUE is not calculated or considered in RI operations.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Familiarise the team with Carbon Usage Effectiveness (CUE) and assess whether sufficient data exists to begin basic calculations. Identify carbon-intensive operational areas.

answerOptions.description: CUE is estimated or partially tracked, but not actively used in decision-making.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement regular CUE calculations and use the results to identify inefficiencies or opportunities for emissions reduction. Integrate CUE into internal sustainability reporting.

answerOptions.description: CUE is regularly calculated, reported, and informs carbon reduction strategies.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain consistent tracking and consider publishing or benchmarking CUE data to support transparency and best practices.

questions.text: Does the RI participate in energy flexibility initiatives, such as demand response or smart grid integration?
questions.associatedMetric: Participation in flexibility programs; % load modulated during events
questions.reference: EU Electricity Directive, EN 50600-4-7 (Availability and resilience), Smart Grid Task Force reports

answerOptions.description: The RI does not participate in any energy flexibility or smart grid schemes.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Explore opportunities to participate in local or regional energy flexibility schemes, such as demand response programmes or grid-interactive energy management. Assess technical feasibility and potential benefits.

answerOptions.description: The RI has the technical capacity and is exploring or piloting flexibility participation.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Participate in one or more energy flexibility initiatives. Establish internal protocols for responding to external grid signals or adjusting loads dynamically.

answerOptions.description: The RI actively participates in energy flexibility schemes and can adjust loads in response to grid signals.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain participation and evaluate performance and value of engagement. Look for additional integration points as technology evolves.

questions.text: Which environmental KPIs (e.g. GHG emissions, energy, water, waste) are currently measured and how frequently are they reported?
questions.associatedMetric: Number and frequency of reported environmental KPIs
questions.reference: ISO 14031, ESRS E1–E5, GHG Protocol, ISO 14064

answerOptions.description: Environmental KPIs are not measured or reported.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify key environmental KPIs relevant to RI operations. Begin collecting basic data on at least one dimension (e.g. energy use or waste generation) and define a reporting cadence.

answerOptions.description: Some KPIs are measured and occasionally reported, but coverage or consistency is limited.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand KPI measurement to cover multiple impact areas (e.g. GHG, energy, water, waste). Establish consistent reporting processes and assign responsibilities.

answerOptions.description: A comprehensive set of environmental KPIs is regularly monitored, documented, and reviewed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue regular monitoring and explore opportunities to align KPI reporting with external frameworks or certifications.

questions.text: To what extent are environmental metrics integrated into the RI’s strategic planning or operational decision-making?
questions.associatedMetric: Number of decisions/plans referencing environmental KPIs (optional, qualitative)

answerOptions.description: Environmental metrics are not used to inform planning or operational decisions.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify opportunities to integrate basic environmental metrics (e.g. energy use, GHG emissions) into routine planning or procurement decisions. Raise awareness among decision-makers.

answerOptions.description: Some metrics are referenced in planning, but not systematically or with limited influence.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Incorporate environmental metrics into key strategic documents, project evaluations, or budget planning. Ensure decision-makers have access to relevant data during planning cycles.

answerOptions.description: Environmental metrics are regularly used to guide strategic and operational decision-making.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain integration and periodically evaluate the influence of metrics on outcomes to ensure environmental performance is driving decisions.

questions.text: Has the RI conducted or applied lifecycle assessments (LCA) to evaluate its environmental impact across different phases, including monitoring of Global Warming Potential (GWP) and Cumulative Energy Demand (CED)?
questions.associatedMetric: Number or coverage of LCA studies conducted

answerOptions.description: No lifecycle assessments have been conducted or considered.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Build internal awareness of LCA methods and identify opportunities to apply LCA to RI infrastructure or service components. Consider piloting an LCA for a specific lifecycle phase or asset.

answerOptions.description: Some LCA activities have been initiated, covering limited operations or infrastructure.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Apply LCA to selected infrastructure or services across multiple lifecycle phases. Use the results to inform design or procurement decisions.

answerOptions.description: LCA is systematically used to assess and mitigate environmental impact across RI lifecycle phases.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue refining LCA approaches and integrating results into sustainability reporting and strategic planning.

questions.text: Is WUE or any comparable metric tracked and used to assess the RI’s water efficiency performance?

answerOptions.description: Water metrics like WUE are not tracked or are unknown.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Review current water usage practices and assess the feasibility of applying Water Usage Effectiveness (WUE) or a comparable metric. Begin collecting relevant data where possible.

answerOptions.description: WUE or a similar metric is estimated periodically but not used in performance management.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement regular tracking of WUE or similar metrics. Use insights to guide efficiency improvements and water-saving initiatives.

answerOptions.description: WUE is regularly calculated, benchmarked, and used to drive technical improvements.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue metric tracking and review performance regularly. Consider benchmarking against best practices or sector peers.

questions.text: Are dedicated tools or platforms in place for tracking, visualising, or reporting environmental performance?

answerOptions.description: No dedicated environmental monitoring tools or platforms are used.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify suitable tools or platforms for environmental data tracking and reporting. Evaluate whether existing systems (e.g. monitoring software, spreadsheets) meet reporting needs.

answerOptions.description: Basic tools exist but are limited in scope or only partially used.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement and configure dedicated tools to track and visualise key environmental indicators. Begin internal reporting and align outputs with decision-making needs.

answerOptions.description: Integrated tools/platforms track and report key indicators across the RI.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain tool usage and explore opportunities for system integration or external disclosure where applicable.

questions.text: Are practices in place to reduce operational waste generation, including e-waste from IT systems and equipment?
questions.associatedMetric: Volume of e-waste or operational waste per year
questions.reference: WEEE Directive, ISO 14001, EU Circular Economy Action Plan

answerOptions.description: No structured practices for waste reduction exist.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify main sources of operational and e-waste. Begin implementing basic waste reduction measures (e.g. paperless processes, proper e-waste disposal procedures).

answerOptions.description: Some informal or partial practices exist for waste minimisation.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Expand and formalise waste reduction initiatives. Introduce structured e-waste handling, asset tracking, and staff awareness programmes.

answerOptions.description: Formal and systematic waste reduction practices are implemented, tracked, and reviewed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain active reduction strategies and consider partnerships for certified e-waste recycling or donation schemes.

questions.text: Does the RI apply defined procedures for the reuse, recycling, or proper disposal of decommissioned equipment?

answerOptions.description: No defined procedures for managing end-of-life equipment.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Develop and document procedures for the proper reuse, recycling, or disposal of decommissioned equipment, in compliance with WEEE or relevant regulations.

answerOptions.description: Some practices exist but are informal or inconsistently applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement procedures and ensure responsible parties follow them consistently. Track decommissioned assets and apply certified disposal or reuse practices.

answerOptions.description: Structured procedures ensure responsible reuse, recycling, or disposal of equipment
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain procedures and consider auditing or reporting results to enhance transparency and continuous improvement.

questions.text: Are circular IT principles such as reuse, modularity, or refurbishment applied in procurement or infrastructure management?
questions.associatedMetric: % of IT purchases meeting circularity criteria
questions.reference: EU Circular Economy Action Plan, ISO 20400 (Sustainable Procurement)

answerOptions.description: Circularity principles are not considered in IT procurement or infrastructure management.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Review procurement and infrastructure policies to identify opportunities to apply circular IT principles (e.g. reuse, modular design, refurbishment). Begin piloting circular approaches in select areas.

answerOptions.description: Some criteria (e.g., reuse, modularity) are considered, but application is limited.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Integrate circular IT requirements into procurement specifications and asset management practices. Collaborate with suppliers to support reuse or refurbishment options.

answerOptions.description: Circular IT principles are systematically embedded into procurement and lifecycle management.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Sustain and refine circular IT practices. Monitor outcomes and explore additional innovation opportunities (e.g. remanufacturing, take-back schemes).

questions.text: Are measures in place to monitor and manage pollution risks related to air, water, or soil emissions from RI operations?
questions.associatedMetric: Presence and scope of pollution monitoring and control measures
questions.reference: ISO 14001, EU Industrial Emissions Directive, ESRS E2

answerOptions.description: No pollution monitoring or control measures are in place.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify potential pollution risks across RI operations (air, water, soil). Begin planning basic monitoring or mitigation actions in line with legal and environmental standards.

answerOptions.description: Some measures exist for specific risks or processes.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement monitoring measures (e.g. emission tracking, discharge controls) and integrate them into operational procedures. Ensure staff are trained on pollution management protocols.

answerOptions.description: Comprehensive monitoring and mitigation measures are in place and reviewed regularly.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain active pollution monitoring and control systems. Conduct periodic reviews and align practices with evolving environmental standards.

questions.text: Are environmental criteria applied when selecting materials or products, with preference for low-impact or non-toxic options?
questions.associatedMetric: % of materials/products procured meeting low-impact criteria
questions.reference: ESPR (Ecodesign), EU Green Public Procurement Criteria, ISO 14024

answerOptions.description: Environmental aspects are not considered in material or product selection.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Introduce environmental criteria into procurement or product selection processes. Begin prioritising low-impact or non-toxic options, especially for high-volume or high-impact categories.

answerOptions.description: Some environmental criteria are applied inconsistently or only for select categories.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Formalise and apply environmental criteria across material/product procurement. Refer to ecolabels or standards (e.g. EU Ecolabel, ISO 14024) where applicable.

answerOptions.description: Environmental criteria are systematically applied across procurement processes.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain current procurement criteria and periodically review for alignment with updated sustainability requirements.

questions.text: To what extent does the RI monitor its water consumption and implement strategies to reduce it?

answerOptions.description: Water consumption is not monitored and no reduction efforts are planned.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Begin monitoring water usage across relevant operations. Identify key consumption points and explore opportunities for reduction (e.g. fixture upgrades, behavioural campaigns).

answerOptions.description: Water use is tracked and some reduction measures are informally applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement structured water management strategies, such as water-efficient technologies or consumption targets. Regularly track and review water usage data.

answerOptions.description: Water use is systematically monitored and targeted reduction plans are implemented and reviewed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain monitoring and review strategies for continuous improvement or integration into broader environmental reporting.

questions.text: Does the RI assess and manage its potential impacts on local ecosystems or biodiversity, particularly for physical infrastructure?
questions.reference: EU Biodiversity Strategy, ISO 14001, ESRS E4

answerOptions.description: Ecosystem or biodiversity impacts are not assessed or considered.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Conduct an initial review of how RI activities or infrastructure may affect local ecosystems or biodiversity. Identify areas of sensitivity and potential impact.

answerOptions.description: Some assessments have been conducted, but mitigation actions are limited.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Apply formal assessment methods (e.g. environmental impact assessments, habitat studies) and implement mitigation strategies to address identified risks.

answerOptions.description: Ecosystem impacts are systematically assessed and managed with appropriate mitigation plans.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Continue managing ecosystem impacts through structured processes and periodic reassessments, particularly when expanding or modifying infrastructure.

questions.text: Are there systems or practices in place for rainwater harvesting, water reuse, or managing stormwater runoff?

answerOptions.description: No rainwater harvesting or runoff management systems are present or considered.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Assess site infrastructure to identify opportunities for rainwater harvesting or stormwater management. Begin feasibility studies or pilot planning for basic systems.

answerOptions.description: Some measures exist but are limited in scope or capacity.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Install rainwater collection or stormwater control systems. Implement reuse strategies (e.g. for irrigation, cooling) where applicable, and maintain performance records.

answerOptions.description: Rainwater harvesting or runoff systems are well-integrated and used for water reuse or management.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain existing systems and review effectiveness periodically. Explore optimisation opportunities or expand reuse capacity.

questions.text: Does the RI use water-based cooling systems, and are measures in place to optimise their water efficiency and minimise environmental impact?
questions.associatedMetric: Water Usage Effectiveness (WUE), annual water withdrawal for cooling
questions.reference: ISO 30134-9, ESRS E3, EU CoC for Data Centres

answerOptions.description: Water-based cooling systems are used, but water consumption is not actively monitored or optimised. No clear strategy exists for minimising impact.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Begin tracking water usage related to cooling systems. Identify inefficiencies and research available technologies for reducing consumption or shifting to closed-loop or hybrid systems.

answerOptions.description: Water consumption for cooling is monitored, and efficiency strategies (e.g. recycling, closed-loop systems) are partially implemented. Environmental considerations are acknowledged but not systematically applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement water-saving strategies such as evaporative cooling optimisation, water reuse, or integration of hybrid cooling. Document performance and begin setting reduction goals.

answerOptions.description: Water efficiency is embedded in cooling system design and operation. WUE is tracked, targets are set, and strategies are actively used to reduce water use and minimise ecological impact.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain current best practices, regularly review WUE performance, and update systems based on evolving best-in-class approaches.

questions.text: To what extent are sustainability considerations integrated into the design and planning of new infrastructure or services?
questions.reference: ISO 14006, ESRS E1, ESPR, EU Taxonomy (Substantial Contribution Criteria)

answerOptions.description: Sustainability is not considered in the design phase.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Introduce sustainability checkpoints into design and planning processes. Begin referencing basic sustainability principles or guidelines during early-stage decision-making.

answerOptions.description: Some sustainability aspects are reviewed, but not systematically.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Embed defined sustainability criteria into design frameworks, including materials selection, energy efficiency, and end-of-life considerations. Document decisions and outcomes.

answerOptions.description: Sustainability is fully embedded in design/planning processes, with clear criteria and review steps.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain integrated sustainability planning and periodically review alignment with updated standards and organisational goals.

questions.text: Are sustainability criteria applied during the implementation or deployment of infrastructure components and systems?

answerOptions.description: No sustainability criteria are applied during implementation.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify implementation phases where sustainability can be applied (e.g. material sourcing, vendor selection, construction). Begin including basic criteria in execution checklists.

answerOptions.description: Some criteria are applied in specific areas, but inconsistently.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Formalise sustainability criteria within procurement and deployment procedures. Monitor compliance and document implementation outcomes.

answerOptions.description: Sustainability criteria are systematically applied during all implementation activities.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain and review implementation practices to ensure continued alignment with sustainability standards and operational objectives.

questions.text: What sustainability practices are in place for the operation and maintenance of the RI’s infrastructure and services?

answerOptions.description: No defined sustainability practices exist during operational phases.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Identify operational areas where sustainability practices can be introduced (e.g. energy management, resource efficiency, preventive maintenance). Begin applying basic principles.

answerOptions.description: Some practices are implemented, but lack consistency or systematic monitoring.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Implement structured sustainability practices into operations and maintenance (e.g. energy tracking, green cleaning protocols, lifecycle extension). Monitor effectiveness and adjust as needed.

answerOptions.description: Sustainability practices are embedded in operations, regularly reviewed, and improved.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain existing practices and explore new opportunities to enhance operational sustainability.

questions.text: Does the RI include environmental considerations in its decommissioning or termination plans for infrastructure or services?

answerOptions.description: Environmental aspects are not addressed during decommissioning.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Review existing decommissioning or termination procedures and identify gaps related to environmental impact (e.g. equipment disposal, site remediation). Draft basic guidelines for responsible end-of-life practices.

answerOptions.description: Some end-of-life measures are in place, but not consistently or formally applied.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: Integrate formal environmental considerations into decommissioning plans, including reuse, recycling, and waste minimisation. Assign responsibilities and track execution.

answerOptions.description: Environmental considerations are embedded in decommissioning plans and routinely followed.
answerOptions.recommendedAction: No action needed. Maintain current decommissioning protocols and review periodically for alignment with best practices and emerging requirements.
